author: codex
scope: Codex-only relation endpoint-shape rule-or-data triage; deterministic read-only inspection plus this single prep artifact; no provider-backed commands, generated-output commands, durable data edits, or code edits

recent_work_not_repeated: relation cleanup batches037-042 for timeline, published_by, implemented_by, and related_route buckets
current_checkpoint_context.inspection_method: Read-only SQLite queries against data/canonical.db using the current RELATION_ENDPOINT_SHAPES table transcribed from packages/pipeline/src/records/relations.ts, plus jq/rg inspection of canonical JSONL, source blocks, and existing prep artifacts.

remaining_bucket_counts.triage_note: All current exact endpoint-shape buckets are singletons. After excluding the user-listed recent rule and data lanes, the selected bucket is the clearest data-modeling error with a safe replacement shape.

recommended_cleanup.mechanism: Retire the malformed relation submission and append replacement submissions in a future cleanup batch; do not patch the validator.
recommended_cleanup.why_data_cleanup: The relation is trying to connect Hudson Line Resilience to the 'Next Up: Planning' planning/program heading on page 20, but its object endpoint is the broader 'MTA C&D Cost Containment Strategy' claim from page 6. Claims are source-stated assertions, not program containers. The current validator correctly allows part_of_program project->project and project->entity, and should not be widened to project->claim for this row.
recommended_cleanup.why_not_validator_rule_patch: Allowing part_of_program project->claim would bless strategy-summary claims as program endpoints. That would blur the boundary between source claims and durable program/project entities and would make future cleanup harder. The source has a better program-like literal, 'Next Up: Planning', that can be represented as a small project/program endpoint and used as the object of a valid project->project relation.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Integrate strategy about what to build & address multiple needs at the same time

payload_direction.description: MTA C&D planning approach to integrate strategy about what to build and address multiple needs at the same time.

evidence_refs.source_quote: 20 miles of integrated Hudson Line resilience & state of good repair

payload_direction.description: Replacement for malformed part_of_program project->claim row; the page presents Hudson Line resilience under the Next Up: Planning heading.

recommended_cleanup.retire_only_fallback: If the future cleanup pass is unwilling to create a small Next Up: Planning project/program endpoint, retire sub_9c7f87794ca04b4a without replacement rather than widening part_of_program to claim objects.

payload_summary.description: Hudson Line resilience is a Next Up: Planning project under the MTA C&D Cost Containment Strategy

evidence.quote: 20 miles of integrated Hudson Line resilience & state of good repair

representative_records.triage_read: The source-backed relation target is the page-20 program/planning heading, not the page-6 cost-containment claim. Replace with a project/program endpoint and a valid part_of_program project->project relation.

Possible future rule/data bucket, but not selected here. The evidence says LIRR planned schedule adjustments and that improvements affect operations through the Main Line corridor; it is weaker as a validator patch than the selected cleanup.
Clear counterexample for broadening operates_on_corridor. The object is an MTA entity while the cited sentence says ABLE began across routes and boroughs.
Not selected because it likely needs route endpoint reconstruction for A and S lines, whereas the selected bucket has an immediate source-backed replacement shape.

source_and_canonical_evidence_summary: The malformed relation, project_hudson-line-resilience, and claim_cost-containment-strategy-4-pillars are canonical records with accepted submission evidence.
The relation cites meeting_doc_165406#p020_c0002, which is the page heading 'Next Up: Planning'. Adjacent page-20 blocks describe the planning strategy and the Hudson Line resilience outcome.

The current object claim cites meeting_doc_165406#p006_c0002, a separate page-6 'Cost Containment Strategy: MTA C&D' heading. It is useful source context, but it is not a valid program endpoint for part_of_program.
A replacement project/program endpoint for 'Next Up: Planning' plus a project->project part_of_program relation would preserve the source-backed hierarchy while satisfying the existing validator.

selected_source_blocks.raw_text: Integrate strategy about what to build & address multiple needs at the same time
20 miles of integrated Hudson Line resilience & state of good repair

validator_boundary.interpretation: The valid shape table already has a suitable project->project path for a program-like endpoint. The incorrect endpoint kind is the data bug.

non_goals: Do not retire, replace, or edit canonical data as part of this prep.
Do not apply a code patch in this artifact-only lane.
Do not rerun materialize, export-jsonl, full validate, DB publish, write, ask, schema, ontology-normalize, identity-review, canonicalize, campaign dedup, or any provider/profile-backed command.
Do not repeat the recent owned_by corridor->entity, operated_by corridor->entity, has_claim treatment_component->claim, or has_treatment route->treatment_component rule patches.
Do not pick another timeline, published_by, implemented_by, or related_route data batch in this next19 triage.
Do not widen part_of_program to accept claim objects based on this row.
Do not point the replacement relation at the broad cost-containment claim merely because the existing malformed row did; use the page-20 Next Up: Planning context or retire-only.

commands_run_read_only: rg -n 'unexpected_relation_endpoint_shape|endpoint_shape|endpoint shape|relation endpoint' data packages docs -g '*.json' -g '*.jsonl' -g '*.ts' -g '*.md'
sqlite3 -readonly data/canonical.db '.tables' '.schema records' '.schema relations' '.schema evidence_refs' '.schema record_submissions'
sqlite3 -readonly -json data/canonical.db '<current endpoint-shape inventory for known relation kinds using current rule table>'
sqlite3 -readonly -header -column data/canonical.db '<current endpoint-shape issue rows with record ids, endpoint kinds, sources, and submissions>'
sqlite3 -readonly -json data/canonical.db '<selected relation, endpoint, and canonical context queries>'
rg -n 'Next Up|Cost Containment|Hudson Line Resilience|project_hudson-line-resilience|claim_cost-containment-strategy-4-pillars' data/canonical data/submissions data/review_notes -S

rg -n 'operates_on_corridor|relation_lirr-operates-main-line|Main Line corridor|entity->corridor|entity -> corridor' data/review_notes data/submissions packages/pipeline/src/records/relations.ts packages/pipeline/test/records/relations.test.ts -S
commands_intentionally_not_run: any command with --profile or provider-backed execution
